ENDORSES

The article uses the framing "operation –њ–Њ –≤–Њ–Ј–≤—А–∞—Й–µ–љ–Є—О –Ъ—А—Л–Љ–∞" (operation to return Crimea), presenting the 2014 annexation as a "return" to Russia rather than a contested territorial seizure, and treats "–†–µ—Б–њ—Г–±–ї–Є–Ї–∞ –Ъ—А—Л–Љ" as an established Russian entity without qualification or dispute markers.

REPORTS

The article reports on a poll about Crimean preferences while using clear framing that rejects Russian sovereignty claims, including labeling the 2014 events as "–Њ–Ї–Ї—Г–њ–Є—А–Њ–≤–∞–ї–Є" (occupied) and "–∞–љ–љ–µ–Ї—Б–Є–Є –Ъ—А—Л–Љ–∞" (annexation of Crimea), and describing territories as "–≤—А–µ–Љ–µ–љ–љ–Њ –Њ–Ї–Ї—Г–њ–Є—А–Њ–≤–∞–љ–љ—Л—Е" (temporarily occupied), which indicates it is reporting on rather than endorsing Russian claims.

REPORTS

The article is a news report quoting Putin's statements about Crimea's "accession" to Russia rather than endorsing it as established fact, as evidenced by the framing as an interview excerpt where Putin explains "reasons" for the action and the conditional language around the referendum ("if Crimeans want...").

UNCLEAR

The article text is corrupted/garbled (appears to be a character encoding error), making it impossible to analyze the actual framing or language used regarding Crimea's sovereignty status.

REPORTS

The article quotes Putin's account of the annexation while maintaining journalistic distance by noting the timeline of events, the initial denials followed by later admissions about Russian military presence, and the disputed referendumвАФframing these as contested claims rather than established facts.

ENDORSES

The article treats Crimea as routine Russian territory by categorizing it under "–†–µ—Б–њ—Г–±–ї–Є–Ї–∞ –Ъ—А—Л–Љ, –†–Њ—Б—Б–Є—П" (Republic of Crimea, Russia) in its geographic tags and metadata without any qualification, dispute notation, or alternative framing.

ENDORSES

The article treats "–†–µ—Б–њ—Г–±–ї–Є–Ї–∞ –Ъ—А—Л–Љ" (Republic of Crimea) as an uncontested Russian administrative unit by referencing "–Ъ—А—Л–Љ—Б—В–∞—В" (Crimean statistics service) as a legitimate Russian statistical body and presenting demographic data about "–Ї—А—Л–Љ—З–∞–љ–Ї–Є" (Crimean women) without any qualification, dispute framing, or alternative sovereignty claims.

ENDORSES

The article presents Crimea as an uncontested part of the Russian Federation ("–≤ —Б–Њ—Б—В–∞–≤–µ –†–Њ—Б—Б–Є–є—Б–Ї–Њ–є –§–µ–і–µ—А–∞—Ж–Є–Є" - "as part of the Russian Federation") and treats it as a normal administrative unit requiring updated maps, without acknowledging any dispute over its sovereignty or international legal status.

ENDORSES

The article treats Crimea as an uncontested Russian territory by casually reporting on a beauty pageant occurring "–љ–∞ –њ–Њ–ї—Г–Њ—Б—В—А–Њ–≤–µ" (on the peninsula) and listing it under "—А–µ—Б–њ—Г–±–ї–Є–Ї–∞ –Ї—А—Л–Љ" (Republic of Crimea) as a normal administrative location without any qualifier, dispute notation, or acknowledgment of contested sovereignty.

ENDORSES

The article uses "–†–µ—Б–њ—Г–±–ї–Є–Ї–∞ –Ъ—А—Л–Љ" (Republic of Crimea) as a straightforward geographic descriptor for Simferopol's location and categorizes the story under Russia without any qualifying language, dispute markers, or acknowledgment of contested sovereignty, thereby normalizing Crimea as Russian territory.

**ENDORSES**

The article consistently frames Crimea's integration into Russia using Russian state terminology and perspective ("–≤–Њ–Ј–≤—А–∞—В–∞ –Ъ—А—Л–Љ–∞ –≤ —Б–Њ—Б—В–∞–≤ –†–Њ—Б—Б–Є–Є" [return of Crimea to Russia], "–≤–Њ—Б—Б–Њ–µ–і–Є–љ–µ–љ–Є–µ —Б –†–Њ—Б—Б–Є–µ–є" [reunification with Russia]), presents the 2014 referendum as legitimate (citing the 96.77% vote), and structures the narrative around Putin's justifications without presenting substantive counterarguments or international legal

REPORTS

The article reports on competing narratives about Crimea's statusвАФdescribing Ukraine's planned counter-film and Yanuyk's statements challenging Russian claims, while also covering Russia's documentary framingвАФrather than endorsing either territorial claim as settled fact.

ENDORSES

The article is written from the Russian state perspective (published on Russian state-aligned Lenta.ru), uses the framing "–њ—А–Є—Б–Њ–µ–і–Є–љ–µ–љ–Є—П –Ъ—А—Л–Љ–∞" (accession/joining of Crimea) as an accomplished fact being celebrated, and presents Russian official narratives about the referendum result (96% support) without any qualifying language, caveats, or alternative perspectivesвАФtreating Crimea's incorporation into Russia as a settled matter worthy of national

ENDORSES

The article uses "–†–µ—Б–њ—Г–±–ї–Є–Ї–∞ –Ъ—А—Л–Љ" (Republic of Crimea) as a straightforward geographic designation when reporting that a Japanese former PM had a scheduled meeting with "the leadership of the Republic of Crimea," presenting it as an established administrative entity without qualification, caveat, or acknowledgment of disputed status.

ENDORSES

The article normalizes Crimea as Russian territory by quoting a Russian official describing how Sevastopol "managed to go to Russia" (—Г–і–∞–ї–Њ—Б—М —Г–є—В–Є –≤ –†–Њ—Б—Б–Є—О) through strategic timing of a referendum, presenting this as established fact rather than a disputed claim, and using "Republic of Crimea" as a standard location tag without any qualifier indicating contested sovereignty.

REPORTS

The article quotes Russian opposition figure Ilya Yashin criticizing Putin's admission of military involvement in Crimea's annexation, framing it as a legal violation and evidence of lying, rather than endorsing Russian sovereignty over the territory.

ENDORSES

The article frames the 2015 referendum as a legitimate expression of Crimean will ("residents of Crimea went to polling stations to express themselves on the referendum question") and describes Russia's actions as a rational response to their "vital interests," while characterizing opposing actors (Kiev authorities, the US) as hesitant or ineffectual, thereby normalizing Crimea's incorporation into Russia rather than presenting it as a disputed territorial claim.

"–С–∞—Е–≤–∞–ї—М—Б—В–≤–Њ –Я—Г—В–Є–љ–∞, –Њ—З–µ–≤–Є–і–љ–Њ, —П–≤–ї—П–µ—В—Б—П —Ж–µ–ї—М—О –Ј–∞–њ—Г–≥–∞—В—М –Ъ–Є–µ–≤. –Ъ—А—Л–Љ –±—Л–ї –њ—А–Є—Б–Њ–µ–і–Є–љ–µ–љ –њ—А–∞–Ї—В–Є—З–µ—Б–Ї–Є –±–µ–Ј –≤–Њ–µ–љ–љ–Њ–≥–Њ —Б–Њ–њ—А–Њ—В–Є–≤–ї–µ–љ–Є—П. –Р –≤–Њ—Б—В–Њ—З–љ–∞—П –£–Ї—А–∞–Є–љ–∞ — –Њ—Б–Њ–±–µ–љ–љ–Њ –њ–Њ—А—В–Њ–≤—Л–є –≥–Њ—А–Њ–і –Ь–∞—А–Є—Г–њ–Њ–ї—М — –і—А—Г–≥–Њ–є –Ї–Њ–ї–µ–љ–Ї–Њ—А. –£–Ї—А–∞–Є–љ—Ж—Л –њ—А–Њ—В–Є–≤ —А–Њ—Б—Б–Є–є—Б–Ї–Њ–≥–Њ –њ—А–Є—Б–Њ–µ–і–Є–љ–µ–љ–Є—П –Є —А–Њ—О—В –Ј–µ–Љ–ї—О, —З—В–Њ–±—Л –Ј–∞—Й–Є—В–Є—В—М—Б—П", — –Њ—В–Љ–µ—З–∞–µ—В –Ъ—Н—А–Њ–ї –£–Є–ї—М—П–Љ—Б. –Ь–љ–Њ–≥–Є–µ –Є–Ј–і–∞–љ–Є—П –њ—Г–±–ї–Є–Ї—Г—О—В —Б—В–∞—В—М–Є –Њ —В–Њ–Љ, —З—В–Њ –Є–Ј–Љ–µ–љ–Є–ї–Њ—Б—М –≤ –Ъ—А—Л–Љ—Г "–Ј–∞ –≥–Њ–і –љ–∞—Е–Њ–ґ–і–µ–љ–Є—П –≤ —А—Г–Ї–∞—Е –Ъ—А–µ–Љ–ї—П" . –Ц–Є–Ј–љ—М –Ъ—А—Л–Љ–∞ –≤–Њ –Љ–љ–Њ–≥–Њ–Љ –Є–Ј–Љ–µ–љ–Є–ї–∞—Б—М, —Г–Ї–∞–Ј—Л–≤–∞–µ—В Euronews. –£–≤–µ–ї–Є—З–Є–ї–Є—Б—М –Ј–∞—А–њ–ї–∞—В—Л, –њ–µ–љ—Б–Є–Є –Є –Ї–∞—З–µ—Б—В–≤–Њ —Б–Њ—Ж–Є–∞–ї—М–љ—Л—Е —Г—Б–ї—Г–≥, –љ–Њ –Є —Г—А–Њ–≤–µ–љ—М –±–µ–Ј—А–∞–±–Њ—В–Є—Ж—Л –Є —Ж–µ–љ—Л –≤—Л—А–Њ—Б–ї–Є —В–Њ–ґ–µ. –¶–µ–љ—Л –љ–∞ –њ–Є—В–∞–љ–Є–µ –≤—Л—А–Њ—Б–ї–Є –њ–Њ—З—В–Є –љ–∞–њ–Њ–ї–Њ–≤–Є–љ—Г, –∞ –Є–љ—Д–ї—П—Ж–Є—П –њ–Њ–і—Б–Ї–Њ—З–Є–ї–∞ –љ–∞ 38 –њ—А–Њ—Ж–µ–љ—В–Њ–≤, –њ–Є—И–µ—В —А–µ—Б—Г—А—Б —Б–Њ —Б—Б—Л–ї–Ї–Њ–є –љ–∞ –Ї—А—Л–Љ—Б–Ї–Є–µ –≤–ї–∞—Б—В–Є. –Я–Є–ї–∞—А –С–Њ–љ–µ (Pilar Bonet) –Є–Ј –Є—Б–њ–∞–љ—Б–Ї–Њ–є El Pais –њ–Є—И–µ—В, —З—В–Њ —В–Њ–≤–∞—А–Њ–≤ –љ–∞—А–Њ–і–љ–Њ–≥–Њ –њ–Њ—В—А–µ–±–ї–µ–љ–Є—П –≤ –Ъ—А—Л–Љ—Г –і–Њ—Б—В–∞—В–Њ—З–љ–Њ, –љ–Њ –њ—А–Њ–і—Г–Ї—В—Л –њ–Є—В–∞–љ–Є—П –њ–Њ—Б—В—Г–њ–∞—О—В –љ–µ—А–µ–≥—Г–ї—П—А–љ–Њ, –Є–Ј-–Ј–∞ –љ–µ—Б–Њ–≤–µ—А—И–µ–љ—Б—В–≤–∞ –Ї–µ—А—З–µ–љ—Б–Ї–Њ–є –њ–µ—А–µ–њ—А–∞–≤—Л –Є –±–ї–Њ–Ї–∞–і—Л —Б–Њ —Б—В–Њ—А–Њ–љ—Л –£–Ї—А–∞–Є–љ—Л. –Т–Њ–і–∞ —П–≤–ї—П–µ—В—Б—П –і–µ—Д–Є—Ж–Є—В–Њ–Љ, —А–µ–Ј–Ї–Њ —Г–њ–∞–ї–Є –і–Њ—Е–Њ–і—Л –Њ—В —В—Г—А–Є–Ј–Љ–∞. –Ъ—А–Њ–Љ–µ —В–Њ–≥–Њ, –Ї—А–Є–Ј–Є—Б –ї–Є—И–Є–ї –Ъ—А—Л–Љ –Њ–±–µ—Й–∞–љ–љ—Л—Е –Ь–Њ—Б–Ї–≤–Њ–є –Є–љ–≤–µ—Б—В–Є—Ж–Є–є, –њ–Є—И–µ—В –С–Њ–љ–µ. "–£–ї–Є—Ж—Л –Є –і–Њ—А–Њ–≥–Є –њ–Њ–ї—Г–Њ—Б—В—А–Њ–≤–∞ –љ–∞—Е–Њ–і—П—В—Б—П –≤ –њ–ї–∞—З–µ–≤–љ–Њ–Љ —Б–Њ—Б—В–Њ—П–љ–Є–Є, —Е–Њ—В—П —З–Є—Б–ї–Њ –∞–≤—В–Њ–Љ–Њ–±–Є–ї–µ–є —Г–≤–µ–ї–Є—З–Є–≤–∞–µ—В—Б—П. –Ґ–∞—В–∞—А—Б–Ї–Њ–µ –Љ–µ–љ—М—И–Є–љ—Б—В–≤–Њ –љ–∞—Е–Њ–і–Є—В—Б—П –њ–Њ–і —Г—Б–Є–ї–µ–љ–љ–Њ–є –Њ—Е—А–∞–љ–Њ–є, –∞ –µ–≥–Њ —А—Г–Ї–Њ–≤–Њ–і–Є—В–µ–ї–Є —Б–Њ—Б–ї–∞–љ—Л –≤ –Ъ–Є–µ–≤ –Є–ї–Є –љ–∞—Е–Њ–і—П—В—Б—П –≤ —В—О—А—М–Љ–µ", — –Ј–∞–Љ–µ—З–∞–µ—В –С–Њ–љ–µ. –Ъ–∞–Ї –≤–Є–і–Є–Љ, "—А–µ–њ—А–µ—Б—Б–Є–Є –≤ –Ъ—А—Л–Љ—Г" — –µ—Й–µ –Њ–і–љ–∞ –±–ї–∞–≥–Њ–і–∞—В–љ–∞—П —В–µ–Љ–∞ –і–ї—П –Ј–∞–њ–∞–і–љ—Л—Е –°–Ь–Ш. –Я–Њ–Љ–Є–Љ–Њ —В–∞—В–∞—А—Б–Ї–Њ–≥–Њ —Д–∞–Ї—В–Њ—А–∞, –Ј–∞–њ–∞–і–љ—Л–µ –ґ—Г—А–љ–∞–ї–Є—Б—В—Л —Г–Ї–∞–Ј—Л–≤–∞—О—В, —З—В–Њ –њ–Њ–і –Ј–∞–њ—А–µ—В–Њ–Љ –љ–∞—Е–Њ–і—П—В—Б—П —Г–Ї—А–∞–Є–љ—Б–Ї–Є–µ —Б–Є–Љ–≤–Њ–ї—Л (–љ–∞–њ—А–Є–Љ–µ—А, —Г–Ї—А–∞–Є–љ—Б–Ї–Є–є —Д–ї–∞–≥) –Є –≤—Л—Б—В—Г–њ–ї–µ–љ–Є—П –ґ—Г—А–љ–∞–ї–Є—Б—В–Њ–≤ —Б –њ—А–Њ—Г–Ї—А–∞–Є–љ—Б–Ї–Є–Љ–Є –ї–Њ–Ј—Г–љ–≥–∞–Љ–Є, –њ–Є—И–µ—В –∞–≥–µ–љ—В—Б—В–≤–Њ "–Ґ–∞–љ—О–≥". "–Ъ—А—Л–Љ — –Њ—Б–Њ–±–µ–љ–љ–Њ–µ –Љ–µ—Б—В–Њ –і–ї—П —А–Њ—Б—Б–Є—П–љ, –њ—А–Њ—Б—В–Њ –њ–Њ—В–Њ–Љ—Г —З—В–Њ –њ–Њ—З—В–Є –Ї–∞–ґ–і—Л–є –≥—А–∞–ґ–і–∞–љ–Є–љ –†–Њ—Б—Б–Є–Є –њ—А–Њ–≤–µ–ї —Б—З–∞—Б—В–ї–Є–≤—Л–µ –і–љ–Є –Њ—В–њ—Г—Б–Ї–∞ –љ–∞ —Б–Њ–ї–љ–µ—З–љ–Њ–Љ –њ–Њ–ї—Г–Њ—Б—В—А–Њ–≤–µ –І–µ—А–љ–Њ–≥–Њ –Љ–Њ—А—П, — –њ–Є—И–µ—В —Б –њ—А–µ—В–µ–љ–Ј–Є—П–Љ–Є –љ–∞ –Ј–љ–∞–љ–Є–µ —А—Г—Б—Б–Ї–Њ–є –і—Г—И–Є –Ш–љ–≥–Њ –Ь–∞–љ–љ—В–Њ–є—Д–µ–ї—М, —А—Г–Ї–Њ–≤–Њ–і–Є—В–µ–ї—М —А—Г—Б—Б–Ї–Њ–є —Б–ї—Г–ґ–±—Л DW-WORLD.DE –≤ –Ї–Њ–ї–Њ–љ–Ї–µ "–Ь–љ–µ–љ–Є–µ". - –° –µ–≥–Њ –њ—А–Є–≤–ї–µ–Ї–∞—В–µ–ї—М–љ—Л–Љ –Ї–ї–Є–Љ–∞—В–Њ–Љ, –Ъ—А—Л–Љ –±—Л–ї –Љ–µ—Б—В–Њ–Љ –Њ—В–і—Л—Е–∞ –Є –њ–Њ–≥—А—Г–ґ–µ–љ–Є—П –≤ —В–Њ—Б–Ї—Г —А—Г—Б—Б–Ї–Є—Е –і–µ—П—В–µ–ї–µ–є –Ї—Г–ї—М—В—Г—А—Л, –љ–∞—З–Є–љ–∞—П —Б 19-–≥–Њ –≤–µ–Ї–∞. –Т —Б–Њ–≤–µ—В—Б–Ї–Є–µ –≤—А–µ–Љ–µ–љ–∞ –Ъ—А—Л–Љ –±—Л–ї —Б–∞–Љ—Л–Љ –њ–Њ–њ—Г–ї—П—А–љ—Л–Љ —В—Г—А–Є—Б—В–Є—З–µ—Б–Ї–Є–Љ –љ–∞–њ—А–∞–≤–ї–µ–љ–Є–µ–Љ –≤ —Б—В—А–∞–љ–µ. –Ф–∞–ґ–µ —В–Њ–≥–і–∞, –Ї–Њ–≥–і–∞ –£–Ї—А–∞–Є–љ–∞ —Б—В–∞–ї–∞ –љ–µ–Ј–∞–≤–Є—Б–Є–Љ–Њ–є, —Б–Њ—В–љ–Є —В—Л—Б—П—З —А–Њ—Б—Б–Є—П–љ –њ–Њ-–њ—А–µ–ґ–љ–µ–Љ—Г —Б—В–µ–Ї–∞–ї–Є—Б—М –≤ –Ъ—А—Л–Љ –љ–∞ –Њ—В–і—Л—Е". REPORTS

The article reports on Russian government claims and economic policies in Crimea while maintaining critical distance through skeptical expert commentary (particularly Zubarevich's assessment that Crimea cannot become self-sufficient) and acknowledges external disputes rather than treating Russian sovereignty as an uncontested fact.

REPORTS

The article reports on Putin's statements and the rally while maintaining critical distance by explicitly stating the annexation "received global condemnation and is still regarded as an illegitimate territorial grab," and describes it as a "takeover" rather than adopting Russian sovereignty framing.

REPORTS

The article explicitly frames Crimea as "contested" and "internationally contested," notes that "Ukrainian and Western observers say was a sham referendum," states that "only a handful of countries...believe Crimea belongs to Russia" while "the rest of the world recognizes the peninsula as a Ukrainian territory," and critically examines the negative consequences of the annexation rather than endorsing Russian sovereignty.

ENDORSES

The article treats Crimea as Russian territory without qualification by discussing Russian state company "–І–µ—А–љ–Њ–Љ–Њ—А–љ–µ—Д—В–µ–≥–∞–Ј" (Black Sea Oil and Gas) developing gas fields "–љ–∞ —В–µ—А—А–Є—В–Њ—А–Є–Є –Ъ—А—Л–Љ–∞" (on the territory of Crimea) as a routine domestic resource development project, with no language indicating disputed sovereignty or alternative claims.

REPORTS

The article critically examines Russian state media's handling of Putin's Crimea claims rather than endorsing Russian sovereigntyвАФit frames Putin's admission as a contradiction to earlier denials and highlights the "awkward test" and lack of "good options" this created for Kremlin journalists, positioning the piece as investigative reporting on propaganda rather than acceptance of Russian territorial claims.

REPORTS

The article quotes Russian officials and documentary makers on Crimea but does not itself assert Crimea's sovereigntyвАФit simply reports their statements within a "week in quotes" format designed to present notable public statements.

ENDORSES

The article treats Crimea as an established Russian territory by casually referencing investment opportunities "in Crimea" without any qualifier, dispute notation, or contextual framing that would indicate contested sovereignty, and categorizes it under Russia and "–†–µ—Б–њ—Г–±–ї–Є–Ї–∞ –Ъ—А—Л–Љ" (Republic of Crimea) as a standard administrative entity.

ENDORSES

The article refers to Crimea as "–њ–Њ–ї—Г–Њ—Б—В—А–Њ–≤ –Ъ—А—Л–Љ" (Crimea peninsula) and uses "—А–µ—Б–њ—Г–±–ї–Є–Ї–∞ –Ї—А—Л–Љ" (Republic of Crimea) as a standard geographic/administrative designation without qualification, qualification, or contextual markers indicating disputed territory, treating it as an established Russian region for routine infrastructure reporting.

ENDORSES

The article uses normalized Russian administrative framing (referring to Crimean cities and institutions as functioning under Russian jurisdiction, mentioning "transition to the Russian economic field," and discussing Russian law applicability) without presenting alternative sovereignty claims or framing this as contested territory.
